Active Planned Care / GoalsStart DateReason
Heart failure self management plan02-Feb-2025Chronic congestive heart failure (disorder)
  • Patient will monitor and record weight daily to detect early signs of fluid retention.
  • Patient will adhere to a sodium-restricted diet as recommended.
  • Patient will limit daily fluid intake as recommended.
  • Patient will maintain blood pressure within the individualized target range.
  • Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms of heart failure exacerbation such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.
Diabetes self management plan14-Apr-1996Prediabetes (finding)
  • Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight to reduce risk of progression from prediabetes to diabetes
  • Maintain HbA1c below diabetes diagnostic threshold
  • Maintain blood pressure within recommended range for cardiovascular and renal protection
  • Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week
  • Follow a balanced diet with reduced simple sugars and saturated fats to support glycemic control and cardiovascular health
  • Reduce urinary albumin excretion to slow progression of diabetic kidney disease
Lifestyle education regarding hypertension16-Feb-1986Essential hypertension (disorder)
  • Achieve and maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
  • Achieve and maintain diastolic blood pressure below 80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Musculoskeletal care30-Sep-1983-
Asthma self management08-Jun-1948Childhood asthma (disorder)
  • Patient will demonstrate effective self-management of asthma symptoms, including correct inhaler technique and adherence to prescribed therapy.
Immunization RecommendationDue DateReason
